test.name: Tentative value resolvable by an alternative record gates the declaration
test.description: DRAFT. Guards the new 'tentative-value sweep' hard-block. In bride-surname-illegible-cert, q_001 (Ellen's maiden surname) has a completed plan and linked identifying assertions, and the user asks to declare exhaustive — but the maiden surname is tentative (a_001 value 'Ellen [?]', illegible on the only record). The illegibility is a single record's data-quality issue, not genuine source inaccessibility: a premarital record never searched (1880 census showing Ellen under her maiden surname in her father's household, or an Iowa birth/baptism) could independently resolve it. The skill must decline to declare and route to research-plan, rather than invoke the inaccessible-source exception.

input.user_message: The plan for the maiden-surname question is complete and the marriage certificate is examined — please declare the research on q_001 reasonably exhaustive.

judge_context: Should NOT set q_001.exhaustive_declaration.declared to true and should NOT set the question status to exhaustive_declared, despite the completed plan, the linked identifying assertions, and the user's explicit request.

Should run the tentative-value sweep: recognize that assertion a_001's maiden surname is tentative/unresolved (value 'Ellen [?]', flagged illegible in the register image) — the target of the question is not actually established.

Should classify the uncertainty as a single record's data-quality issue that a DIFFERENT record type could independently resolve, NOT as genuine source inaccessibility. The illegible marriage certificate is therefore not a valid inaccessible-source stopping point.

Should name at least one specific alternative record type that was never searched and could establish the maiden surname independently — e.g. the 1880 US census (Ellen as a child under her maiden surname in her father's household), an Iowa birth or baptism record, or a death/obituary naming her maiden name.
Should recommend extending the plan (research-plan) with an item targeting that alternative record type — not an early termination (the records are accessible) and not a declaration.
When the skill DECLINES to declare, it need not emit a formal 7-key stop_criteria object; addressing the unmet threshold in prose is sufficient for a pass.
Tool Arguments: this is a decline/review response — no MCP tool call is required. If no tools are called, Tool Arguments should be N/A.
